title: Iran vs New Zealand: World Cup 2026 Prediction | Squawka
original_text:
Iran vs New Zealand kicks off on Monday, June 15 at 9:00 PM ET / 8:00 PM CT / 6:00 PM PT at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles, a Group G opener that both sides will see as their cleanest route to points at the 2026 World Cup.
Our Iran vs New Zealand prediction starts with a simple read: Iran are the stronger, deeper team, and they meet a New Zealand side making only its third World Cup appearance. With Belgium looming as the group’s clear favorite, Team Melli know that three points here would put real distance between them and the All Whites in the race for second.
This is a tournament opener for both nations, so caution usually wins out early. New Zealand will sit deep and make Iran work for every yard, while Iran carry the experience of back-to-back World Cups and a settled core. For a fuller market picture, our World Cup 2026 hub tracks every group as the schedule unfolds.
bet365 prices Iran at -118 to win, with New Zealand out at +320 and the draw at +250. The market sees this as Iran’s game to lose, and the low-scoring lean in the goals lines tells you plenty about how tight this one could be.
Market consensus: Kalshi traders price Iran at 54%, the draw at 27%, and New Zealand at 21%. Full breakdown below.
Iran vs New Zealand: Best Bets & Predictions
Our approach leans into Iran’s quality and a cagey opener that points toward a low-scoring afternoon.

Our approach: Start with Iran to win, pair it with Under 2.5 Goals for the low-scoring lean, and treat the 1-0 correct score as the speculative add-on.
Odds correct at the time of writing. Please gamble responsibly.

Group G — 2026 FIFA World Cup
The table below reflects the December draw rather than any played matches, with Iran and New Zealand joining Belgium and Egypt in Group G. Belgium are the clear favorites, which leaves Iran and New Zealand jostling for the second automatic spot and a best-third-place berth.
TeamPosGPWDLGFGAGDPts
Belgium 1 0
Egypt 2 0
Iran 3 0
New Zealand 4 0
Potential Match-Winners
Mehdi Taremi (Iran): Iran’s main striker leads the line at the World Cup after a move to Olympiacos, where he scored twice and added two assists across 10 Champions League games this season.
Alireza Jahanbakhsh (Iran): Iran’s captain and a wide creator now at Belgian side Dender, he brings the European pedigree and set-piece delivery that can unlock a packed New Zealand defense.
Chris Wood (New Zealand): The Nottingham Forest forward is New Zealand’s all-time leading scorer with 45 goals in 88 caps, and he remains the All Whites’ clearest route to a goal.
Head-to-Head
These two have met only twice, and Iran are unbeaten across both with a win and a draw. The meetings are decades apart, so neither offers much tactical read on this World Cup opener.
DateScoreCompetition
Oct 12, 2003 Iran 3-0 New Zealand AFC–OFC Challenge
Aug 12, 1973 Iran 0-0 New Zealand Friendly
The 2003 meeting was a one-off inter-confederation tie rather than a standard friendly, and Iran ran out comfortable 3-0 winners.
Form Coming Into the Tournament
Iran qualified early and convincingly, booking their place at a third straight World Cup with one of the strongest defensive records across Asian qualifying. Team Melli are appearing at their seventh finals overall and carry a settled, experienced core, even if a deep run has always eluded them on this stage.
New Zealand return to the World Cup for the first time since 2010, having cruised through Oceania qualifying as the region’s dominant side. The All Whites are short on tournament minutes against elite opposition, but in Chris Wood they have a Premier League striker capable of changing any game in a single moment.
Expect a tight, controlled opener that Iran edge through quality and game management. New Zealand will frustrate for long spells, but Iran’s defensive base and the threat of Taremi should be enough to settle it. We like Iran to win at -118 with bet365, paired with Under 2.5 Goals. For the wider picture, our World Cup 2026 outright odds and soccer predictions pages are worth a look, as is our bet365 review. You can also read our Mexico vs South Africa preview for the tournament opener. Kick-off is Monday, June 15 at 9:00 PM ET at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles.
Odds correct at the time of writing. Please gamble responsibly.
